title: Structural diff between two prolly trees by root hash comparison
purpose: Implement the structural diff operation that walks two prolly trees simultaneously, skipping subtrees whose root hash matches, and yields only the entries that differ. The cost of the diff is O(differences), not O(total tree size) — this is a direct consequence of P4 (unmodified subtrees are free). The diff operation is the foundation that the branching cluster's merge operation will build on; without it, comparing two database snapshots requires a full scan. A debugging developer must be able to diff two large database states that differ in a handful of keys and receive results in sub-millisecond time.
task: Create crates/haematite/src/tree/diff.rs implementing a diff function that takes a NodeStore reference and two root hashes, walks both trees in parallel by key order, and yields DiffEntry items for each key that differs. DiffEntry has three variants: Added(key, value) when a key exists only in tree B, Removed(key, value) when a key exists only in tree A, and Modified(key, old_value, new_value) when a key exists in both trees with different values. The critical invariant: when two nodes at the same tree position share a hash, the function MUST NOT descend into them — the entire subtree is identical and can be skipped. This brief covers the diff algorithm and its types only. Branching, merging, and three-way merge are out of scope — those belong to the branching cluster.

requirements.title: DiffEntry type with Added, Removed, and Modified variants
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a DiffEntry enum with three variants: Added { key: Vec<u8>, value: Vec<u8> }, Removed { key: Vec<u8>, value: Vec<u8> }, and Modified { key: Vec<u8>, old_value: Vec<u8>, new_value: Vec<u8> }. THE SYSTEM SHALL implement Debug and PartialEq on DiffEntry. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT include hash values in DiffEntry — callers work with keys and values, not internal tree identifiers.
acceptance: DiffEntry::Added { key, value } is constructible
DiffEntry::Removed { key, value } is constructible
DiffEntry::Modified { key, old_value, new_value } is constructible
DiffEntry implements Debug
DiffEntry implements PartialEq so tests can use assert_eq!
DiffEntry fields use Vec<u8> for keys and values — no hash fields present

files: crates/haematite/src/tree/diff.rs:1-1 — currently only a CORE-003 comment; define `DiffEntry` here.
crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:9-10 — `Hash` is the internal content identifier; keep it out of `DiffEntry`.
crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:62-75 — `LeafNode` stores sorted `Vec<(Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>)>`, matching the required owned key/value field types.
crates/haematite/src/tree/cursor.rs:8-23 — public error/type convention: derive `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq`, implement `Display`, and implement `std::error::Error` for error types.
context: Public types in this crate derive `Debug`; comparable public enums such as `TreeError` also derive `Clone, PartialEq, Eq`, which is useful even though the brief only requires `PartialEq`.
Use struct variants exactly as specified: `Added { key, value }`, `Removed { key, value }`, and `Modified { key, old_value, new_value }`.
`LeafNode::entries()` returns borrowed owned pairs, so clone keys/values only when emitting a diff entry.
Production code should not use `.unwrap()`/`.expect()`; keep those, if needed, in tests only.
scout.approach: In `crates/haematite/src/tree/diff.rs`, define `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)] pub enum DiffEntry` with exactly the three struct variants specified and only `Vec<u8>` fields. Add focused tests that construct each variant and use `assert_eq!`/`format!("{entry:?}")` to exercise the derives without needing traversal. Do not include `Hash` in this enum; hashes belong only in traversal inputs and error reporting.
scout.notes: Because `diff.rs` will also hold the algorithm and tests, watch CN1’s 500-line limit. If it gets close, prefer moving integration-style tests to `crates/haematite/tests/core003.rs` rather than putting logic in `mod.rs`.

files_changed.note: Defined public DiffEntry with Added, Removed, and Modified struct variants using Vec<u8> fields; added focused unit coverage for construction, Debug, and PartialEq.
Added integration tests that construct and compare DiffEntry values through diff results.

dev.how: DiffEntry is now a public enum deriving Debug, Clone, PartialEq, and Eq with exactly the required caller-facing key/value fields and no hash fields. The implementation clones keys/values only when emitting entries from leaf scans.

checklist.note: DiffEntry is the emitted item type for the implemented structural diff walker.

stories.note: Diff output now expresses added, removed, and modified keys/values for comparing two database states.

requirements.title: diff function signature and NodeStore integration
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL expose a diff function with the signature diff<S: NodeStore>(store: &S, root_a: &Hash, root_b: &Hash) -> Result<Vec<DiffEntry>, DiffError>. WHEN root_a and root_b are equal, THE SYSTEM SHALL return an empty Vec without loading any nodes from the store. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT require the caller to hold the trees in memory — all node access MUST go through the NodeStore trait defined in CORE-001. IF a required node hash is not found in the store, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return DiffError::MissingNode(hash).
acceptance: diff(store, hash, hash) where both hashes are identical returns Ok(vec![]) without any store.get calls
diff(store, root_a, root_b) returns Ok(entries) when both roots are reachable
diff(store, root_a, root_b) returns Err(DiffError::MissingNode(h)) when a node at hash h is absent from the store
DiffError implements std::error::Error and Debug
The function accepts any S: NodeStore — it is not coupled to MemoryStore

files: crates/haematite/src/store/memory.rs:7-13 — `NodeStore` trait signature; all node access must go through `get(&Hash)`.
crates/haematite/src/tree/cursor.rs:231-236 — existing node-loading helper maps store `None` into `TreeError::MissingNode`; mirror this behavior for `DiffError`.
crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:152-180 — `Node` enum and `hash()` behavior used after loading roots/children.
crates/haematite/tests/core002.rs:11-49 — `CountingStore` pattern for asserting `get` call counts and generic `NodeStore` usage.
context: The public function should be `pub fn diff<S: NodeStore + ?Sized>(store: &S, root_a: &Hash, root_b: &Hash) -> Result<Vec<DiffEntry>, DiffError>`; `?Sized` keeps it usable with trait objects while still satisfying `S: NodeStore` callers.
The root equality fast path must run before any store access: `if root_a == root_b { return Ok(Vec::new()); }`.

`NodeStore::get` returns `Result<Option<Node>, S::Error>` but the required `DiffError` is not generic. Decide on a non-generic store-read variant or collapse read errors deliberately; absence must become `DiffError::MissingNode(hash)`.
`Hash` is `Copy`, so storing the missing hash by value in `DiffError` is straightforward.
scout.approach: Create `DiffError` in `diff.rs` with at least `MissingNode(Hash)`, derive `Debug`, implement `Display`, and implement `std::error::Error`. Add a private `load_diff_node` that calls `store.get(hash)`, returns the node on `Ok(Some(_))`, returns `DiffError::MissingNode(*hash)` on `Ok(None)`, and handles store errors via a non-generic variant if needed. Then implement `diff` with the no-read equality fast path and delegate to a private recursive/stack walker that appends to a `Vec<DiffEntry>`.
scout.notes: The brief phrases acceptance as `DiffError::MissingNode(h)`, so tuple-style is safer than matching `TreeError`’s struct-style `MissingNode { hash }`. Custom counting stores must implement/derive `Debug` because `NodeStore: Debug`.

files_changed.note: Added DiffError and public diff<S: NodeStore + ?Sized>(store, root_a, root_b) -> Result<Vec<DiffEntry>, DiffError> with the root-equality no-read fast path and NodeStore-only node loading.
Added tests for no reads on equal roots, missing-node reporting, generic NodeStore/trait-object use, and reachable roots.

dev.how: The public diff function first returns Ok(Vec::new()) when root hashes match, before any store access. All node loads go through a private load_diff_node helper using NodeStore::get; absent nodes return DiffError::MissingNode(hash), and DiffError implements Debug, Display, and std::error::Error.
dev.deviation: DiffError includes two additional non-generic variants, StoreRead and InvalidNode, to keep the required non-generic diff signature while still handling fallible NodeStore reads and malformed internal nodes without panicking.

requirements.title: Subtree skipping when hashes match (P4)
requirements.spec: WHILE walking both trees simultaneously, WHEN the current node hash in tree A equals the current node hash in tree B at the same position, THE SYSTEM SHALL skip that subtree entirely and produce no DiffEntry items for any key beneath it. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT call store.get on a node whose hash matches the opposing tree's node hash at the same position — descending is prohibited when hashes match. This is P4: unmodified subtrees are free.
acceptance: A store that panics on get for shared-hash nodes does not panic during diff of two trees that share a subtree
diff of two trees that share all subtrees except one leaf returns only the entries from that leaf — not entries from shared subtrees
store.get is not called for any node whose hash appears identically on both sides of the comparison at the same tree position
A test with a 1000-entry tree where one key is modified records fewer than 20 store.get calls

files: crates/haematite/src/tree/diff.rs:1-1 — implement subtree skip checks at the start of every pair comparison here.
crates/haematite/src/tree/mutate.rs:162-171 — unchanged children are re-used by hash when internal nodes are rewritten.
crates/haematite/src/tree/mutate.rs:312-334 — root rebuilding flattens/reuses existing subtrees, enabling low read counts after localized changes.
crates/haematite/tests/core002.rs:283-296 — existing test confirms unchanged sibling subtrees retain the same hash after mutation.
context: The skip rule is recursive, not root-only: whenever paired hashes are equal, return immediately and do not call `store.get` for either hash.
Internal/internal traversal must compare aligned child ranges and skip equal child hashes before loading children.
Do not assume child index equality alone always means the same key range; child separator keys are lower bounds, not upper bounds.
A panic-on-shared-hash test store is useful to catch accidental descent into shared subtrees.
scout.approach: Make the core comparison helper take `(hash_a, hash_b)` and begin with `if hash_a == hash_b { return Ok(()); }`. Only after that should it load nodes and dispatch on leaf/leaf, internal/internal, and asymmetric cases. For internal/internal, merge child references in key order using their separator ranges and recurse only on differing aligned ranges; never load an equal child-hash pair. Add tests with manually shared subtree hashes and with a large mostly-shared tree to verify read counts stay low.
scout.notes: “Same position” is important: equal hashes observed in unrelated ranges should not be skipped unless the traversal has aligned their key ranges. Misinterpreting separators as upper bounds is the likely time sink.

files_changed.note: Implemented recursive pairwise comparison that returns immediately when paired hashes are equal, plus internal/internal child-range alignment by separator ranges.
Added panic-on-shared-hash and read-count tests for shared subtrees, including a 1000-entry one-change tree with fewer than 20 reads.

dev.how: Every recursive comparison starts with a hash-equality check before loading either node. Internal/internal traversal aligns child ranges using lower-bound separators, recursing only for differing aligned ranges and thereby preserving the same-position skip invariant.

checklist.note: Equal paired child hashes are skipped before any store.get call.
Read-count tests demonstrate localized diffs fetch only the differing paths rather than all leaves.

stories.note: Shared subtrees are treated as proof of identity, so unchanged regions are effectively free.

requirements.title: Parallel in-order traversal of leaf entries
requirements.spec: WHEN both trees descend to leaf nodes at the same position, THE SYSTEM SHALL merge their key-value entry lists with a two-pointer scan in sorted key order. WHEN a key exists in leaf A but not leaf B, THE SYSTEM SHALL yield DiffEntry::Removed. WHEN a key exists in leaf B but not leaf A, THE SYSTEM SHALL yield DiffEntry::Added. WHEN a key exists in both leaves with differing values, THE SYSTEM SHALL yield DiffEntry::Modified. WHEN a key exists in both leaves with identical values, THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT yield any DiffEntry for that key.
acceptance: diff of leaf A = [(b, 2), (c, 3)] and leaf B = [(a, 1), (c, 3)] yields [Added(a,1), Removed(b,2)] in key order
diff of leaf A = [(k, old)] and leaf B = [(k, new)] yields [Modified(k, old, new)]
diff of leaf A = [(k, v)] and leaf B = [(k, v)] yields [] — identical values produce no entry
diff results are in ascending key order

files: crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:62-75 — `LeafNode::new` validates sorted entries and `entries()` returns the sorted slice to scan.
crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:183-198 — sorted-unique validation means the leaf scan does not need duplicate handling.
crates/haematite/tests/core002.rs:55-65 — helper style for storing manual leaf roots from byte slices in tests.
crates/haematite/src/tree/cursor.rs:251-263 — existing code clones values at the public return boundary; follow that pattern for diff entries.
context: Compare keys by byte order with `key_a.as_slice().cmp(key_b.as_slice())`.
The simple leaf/leaf output order should be naturally ascending; the acceptance example expects `Added(a)` before `Removed(b)`.
Identical values suppress output even when the surrounding path hashes differ.
Manual test leaves must be sorted or `LeafNode::new` will return `NodeError::UnsortedKeys`.
scout.approach: Implement a private `diff_leaf_entries(a, b, output)` with a two-pointer scan. On A-only push `Removed`, on B-only push `Added`, on shared key push `Modified` only when values differ, and then drain tails. Use clones only when pushing `DiffEntry`. Add leaf-only tests for added/removed ordering, modified values, identical values, and final ascending order.
scout.notes: Do not sort the final diff vector as a workaround; that can hide traversal-order bugs and add unnecessary cost. Internal traversal should call leaf scans in key order.

files_changed.note: Added private two-pointer leaf scan over sorted entries, producing Added, Removed, Modified, and suppressing identical values.
Added leaf-only acceptance tests for added/removed order, modified values, identical-value suppression, and sorted output.

dev.how: Leaf/leaf comparisons use sorted key order with peekable two-pointer iteration. A-only entries emit Removed, B-only entries emit Added, shared keys emit Modified only when values differ, and no final sorting is used.

checklist.note: Leaf scans produce only key/value differences in ascending key order.

stories.note: Developers can see exact per-key additions, removals, and modifications between roots.

acceptance.evidence: Two-pointer scan compares key_a/key_b and advances in sorted order at crates/haematite/src/tree/diff.rs:253-309; leaf_diff_uses_sorted_two_pointer_scan verifies Added(a) precedes Removed(b) at crates/haematite/tests/core003.rs:206-218.

requirements.title: Asymmetric descent when one side is a leaf and the other is internal
requirements.spec: WHEN one tree has a leaf node at a given position and the other has an internal node (due to structural divergence from unequal key sets), THE SYSTEM SHALL expand the internal node by descending its children while treating the leaf as a terminal on its side. THE SYSTEM SHALL yield Added or Removed entries for all keys reachable under the internal subtree that are absent from the leaf side. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT assume both trees are structurally identical — prolly tree boundaries are content-determined and two trees with different key sets will have different node shapes.
acceptance: diff where tree A has a single leaf with keys [a, b] and tree B has an internal node whose children span [a, b, c, d] yields Added entries for c and d
diff where tree A has keys [a, b, c] as one leaf and tree B has the same keys split across two leaves under an internal node yields []
No panic or error occurs when node depth differs between the two trees at a given key range

files: crates/haematite/src/tree/mutate.rs:258-279 — child separators are first keys in child subtrees; asymmetric traversal must use that lower-bound convention.
crates/haematite/src/tree/cursor.rs:238-249 — `child_index_for_key` documents how separator lower bounds route keys.
crates/haematite/src/tree/cursor.rs:129-176 — range iteration descends internals left-to-right; useful model for collecting subtree entries in sorted order.
crates/haematite/tests/core002.rs:67-77 — manual `store_internal` helper style for shape-divergence tests.
context: Tree shapes can diverge for different key sets; do not zip children by index and assume identical structure.
A child covers `[separator_i, separator_{i+1})`, with the last child extending to the parent range end.
For leaf-vs-internal, polarity matters: keys only in B are `Added`, keys only in A are `Removed`.
Flattening an internal subtree to entries is simple and correct for R5 acceptance, but can hurt proportionality if used in common internal/internal paths.
scout.approach: Add asymmetric helpers that compare a terminal leaf against an internal subtree while preserving sorted order and polarity. The robust starting point is to collect the internal subtree’s leaf entries left-to-right and reuse the leaf two-pointer scan; if keeping read counts tighter, partition the terminal leaf entries by each child’s separator range and recurse per child. Test A leaf `[a,b]` vs B internal covering `[a,b,c,d]`, and A leaf `[a,b,c]` vs B split across two leaves, expecting no differences in the latter.
scout.notes: The expensive gotcha is treating structural divergence as an error. A leaf/internal pair is valid and should be compared by key space, not by node shape.

files_changed.note: Added leaf/internal and internal/leaf asymmetric comparison helpers that collect the internal side in key order and reuse the leaf scan with correct polarity.
Added shape-divergence tests for leaf vs internal added keys and equal key sets split across different depths.

dev.how: When one side is terminal and the other is internal, the implementation expands the internal subtree left-to-right through NodeStore and compares those entries against the leaf entries with the same diff semantics. This avoids assuming identical tree shape and preserves Added/Removed polarity.

checklist.note: Asymmetric shapes are compared by key/value content rather than rejected.
The optimized internal/internal path remains range-aligned and hash-skipping; asymmetric expansion is limited to actual leaf/internal shape divergence.

stories.note: Shape-divergent roots can still be diffed for exact key changes.
Common shared internal paths retain hash-skip behavior; asymmetric expansion is used only where depths differ.

requirements.title: Wire diff module into tree module root and crate public API
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL add diff as a public module in crates/haematite/src/tree/mod.rs using a pub mod declaration. THE SYSTEM SHALL re-export diff, DiffEntry, and DiffError from the crate root lib.rs. tree/mod.rs SHALL NOT contain logic — only pub mod diff and the existing pub mod declarations from CORE-001 and CORE-002.

haematite::diff is callable from outside the crate
haematite::DiffEntry is accessible from outside the crate
haematite::DiffError is accessible from outside the crate
cargo check -p haematite succeeds

files: crates/haematite/src/tree/mod.rs:1-10 — already has `pub mod diff;` and only module declarations/re-exports; add a `pub use diff::{diff, DiffEntry, DiffError};` re-export here if desired.
crates/haematite/src/lib.rs:17-20 — crate root currently re-exports tree APIs; include `diff`, `DiffEntry`, and `DiffError` here.

context: `tree/mod.rs` already satisfies the `pub mod diff` portion and follows CN3; keep it logic-free.
The crate root uses a grouped `pub use tree::{ ... }`; add the new names there rather than introducing an inconsistent export style.
A function named `diff` can be re-exported alongside a module named `diff`; at the crate root, `haematite::diff` should resolve to the function if directly re-exported.
`cargo check -p haematite` is the acceptance check for API wiring.
scout.approach: In `tree/mod.rs`, add `pub use diff::{DiffEntry, DiffError, diff};` next to the other tree re-exports. In `lib.rs`, add `diff, DiffEntry, DiffError` to the existing `pub use tree::{...}` group. Add an integration compile test that imports `haematite::{diff, DiffEntry, DiffError}` and calls `diff` against a `MemoryStore` root pair.
scout.notes: If `haematite::diff` is ambiguous in a test because of the module/function name overlap, explicitly confirm the root re-export points to the function, not only the module.

Added diff, DiffEntry, and DiffError to the crate-root tree re-export list.
Added compile/runtime coverage importing haematite::{diff, DiffEntry, DiffError} from outside the crate.

dev.how: The diff module was already publicly declared; tree and crate root re-exports now make haematite::diff callable and haematite::{DiffEntry, DiffError} accessible externally. tree/mod.rs remains logic-free.

checklist.note: The structural diff function is available through the public crate API.

stories.note: External callers can compare committed root hashes through haematite::diff.

acceptance.criterion: crates/haematite/src/tree/mod.rs contains pub mod diff and no logic
acceptance.evidence: tree/mod.rs contains only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports, including pub mod diff at crates/haematite/src/tree/mod.rs:1-11.

acceptance.criterion: haematite::diff is callable from outside the crate
acceptance.evidence: crate root explicitly re-exports diff at crates/haematite/src/lib.rs:21 after harden fix; integration test imports and calls diff at crates/haematite/tests/core003.rs:4-7 and 166-172.

acceptance.criterion: haematite::DiffError is accessible from outside the crate
acceptance.evidence: crate root explicitly re-exports DiffError at crates/haematite/src/lib.rs:21 after harden fix; integration test imports DiffError and checks std::error::Error at crates/haematite/tests/core003.rs:4-7 and 161-164.

acceptance.criterion: cargo check -p haematite succeeds
acceptance.evidence: Final verification command `cargo check -p haematite` passed with `Finished dev profile` and no errors.

issues: The crate-root re-export was originally semantically present but split across a multi-line pub use; the brief's exact verification command `grep 'pub use' crates/haematite/src/lib.rs | grep -E 'diff|DiffEntry|DiffError'` initially failed because the `pub use` line itself did not contain the diff symbols.
fixes: Added explicit `pub use tree::{DiffEntry, DiffError, diff};` at crates/haematite/src/lib.rs:21 so both the public API and the brief's grep verification are satisfied.

requirements.title: Cost proportionality test: diff is O(differences) not O(tree size)
requirements.spec: WHEN two trees share all but a small number of differing keys, THE SYSTEM SHALL complete the diff in time proportional to the number of differing keys, not the total number of keys. The store.get call count provides the observable proxy for this: the number of nodes fetched SHALL NOT grow linearly with the total key count when the difference count is held constant. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT traverse shared subtrees to verify this — hash equality at a node is sufficient proof of subtree identity (P1).
acceptance: A test builds two trees each containing 10 000 entries where exactly 3 keys differ, runs diff, and asserts the DiffEntry count is 3
The same test asserts that store.get was called fewer than 100 times (substantially less than 10 000)
A test builds two entirely disjoint trees of 100 entries each and asserts diff returns 200 entries (100 Added, 100 Removed)
diff on two empty trees (empty root hashes represented as the hash of an empty node) returns []

files: crates/haematite/tests/core002.rs:11-49 — copy/adapt `CountingStore` to observe `store.get` calls.
crates/haematite/tests/core002.rs:79-81 — empty root convention is a stored empty `LeafNode`, not a sentinel hash.
crates/haematite/tests/core002.rs:91-129 — key generators for boundary/non-boundary cases; boundary-heavy keys help build many leaves/subtrees.
crates/haematite/src/tree/mutate.rs:41-57 — `batch_mutate` builds canonical roots in one call and avoids noisy per-insert history.
crates/haematite/src/tree/boundary.rs:17-43 — boundaries are hash/modulo based; choose keys deliberately when forcing tree splits.
context: Build both roots in the same store, or otherwise merge reachable nodes into one `NodeStore`; `diff` receives only one store.
Reset the read counter after building/mutating roots and before calling `diff`.
For 10,000 entries with 3 differences, use `batch_mutate` for the base and derive the second root with exactly 3 changed keys so most subtrees remain shared.
For the disjoint 100-entry test, assert 100 `Added` and 100 `Removed`, not just total length 200.
scout.approach: Add cost tests after the walker is correct. Create an empty root via `LeafNode::new(Vec::new())`, batch-insert 10,000 sorted entries, mutate exactly 3 existing keys to create root B, reset counting, run `diff`, and assert `entries.len() == 3` and `read_count < 100`. Add separate tests for two disjoint 100-entry roots producing 200 entries and for two empty roots returning `[]`.
scout.notes: If all generated keys land in one huge leaf, read count may look fine while the algorithm scans too much. Use boundary-aware keys or enough entries/values to force a multi-leaf tree so the test actually proves subtree skipping.

files_changed.note: Implemented range-aligned internal traversal and hash-equality skips that keep read counts proportional to changed subtrees.
Added 10,000-entry/3-difference read-count test under 100 gets, disjoint 100+100 test, and empty-root test.

dev.how: The walker skips equal hashes without verification reads and aligns internal child ranges so only differing subtrees are loaded. Tests build large shared trees in one store, reset read counts before diff, assert exactly three entries for three changes with fewer than 100 reads, verify disjoint trees produce 100 Added and 100 Removed entries, and verify empty roots diff to an empty vector.
dev.deviation: For deterministic and fast acceptance coverage, the large proportionality tests use manually chunked multi-leaf trees in the same NodeStore rather than relying on probabilistic boundary-key generation; the production diff algorithm still operates solely on NodeStore-loaded prolly nodes and their separator ranges.

checklist.note: 10,000-entry/3-change and 1000-entry/1-change tests verify low store.get counts compared with tree size.

stories.note: Observable store.get counts stay bounded by changed paths/subtrees rather than total key count.

acceptance.criterion: A test builds two trees each containing 10 000 entries where exactly 3 keys differ, runs diff, and asserts the DiffEntry count is 3
acceptance.evidence: ten_thousand_entries_with_three_differences_reads_under_threshold builds 10,000-entry trees with three changed indices and asserts entries.len() == 3 at crates/haematite/tests/core003.rs:327-341.

acceptance.criterion: The same test asserts that store.get was called fewer than 100 times (substantially less than 10 000)
acceptance.evidence: The same test asserts store.read_count() < 100 at crates/haematite/tests/core003.rs:348-352.

acceptance.criterion: A test builds two entirely disjoint trees of 100 entries each and asserts diff returns 200 entries (100 Added, 100 Removed)
acceptance.evidence: disjoint_trees_report_every_added_and_removed_entry builds 100-entry disjoint trees and asserts len 200, added 100, removed 100 at crates/haematite/tests/core003.rs:356-375.

acceptance.criterion: diff on two empty trees (empty root hashes represented as the hash of an empty node) returns []
acceptance.evidence: empty_tree_diff_is_empty builds two empty roots and asserts diff returns Vec::new() at crates/haematite/tests/core003.rs:377-385.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement three-way merge or conflict resolution — those belong to the branching cluster
SHALL NOT implement fork or branch creation — those belong to the branching cluster
SHALL NOT implement WAL buffer diffing — diff operates on committed tree roots only
SHALL NOT implement streaming or async iteration — diff returns a collected Vec<DiffEntry>
SHALL NOT implement cross-shard diff — each call to diff operates on a single shard's tree; multi-shard coordination is the caller's responsibility
SHALL NOT introduce new serialisation formats — node loading uses the NodeStore trait from CORE-001 exclusively
verification: cargo check -p haematite succeeds with no errors
cargo test -p haematite --lib passes all tests
cargo clippy -p haematite produces no errors
grep -r 'unwrap()\|expect(' crates/haematite/src/tree/diff.rs finds no occurrences outside #[cfg(test)] blocks
wc -l crates/haematite/src/tree/diff.rs reports fewer than 500 lines
grep 'pub mod diff' crates/haematite/src/tree/mod.rs confirms the module is wired
grep 'pub use' crates/haematite/src/lib.rs | grep -E 'diff|DiffEntry|DiffError' confirms all three are re-exported
